Dynamic behavioral adaptation engine based on TARS.

Local Model Help Desk

  • Vision (Optic Nerve): Requires minicpm-v model installed in Ollama.
  • Main Brain: Recommended model is qwythos-9b for stability.
  • OS Permissions: Using local models grants Luna access to your file system and screen.
  • Fixing Issues: If Luna is unresponsive, ensure the Ollama app is running in your Windows tray.

CREATE YOUR IDENTITY

— OR —

CHOOSE YOUR ENGINE

Do you want to run Luna using the fast Cloud APIs (Firebase/Gemini), or run her entirely offline using your own hardware?

How does Local AI work?

Running a local AI means your computer is doing all the thinking. No data is sent to the cloud, it's 100% private, and completely free! We use an incredible tool called Ollama that lets you run large language models on your own machine.

Setup Instructions (Step-by-Step):

  1. Download Ollama: Install Ollama for Windows.
  2. Open Command Prompt: Press Win+R, type cmd, and press Enter.
  3. Download the Brain: Paste this command and hit Enter to download qwythos-9b (this may take a few minutes depending on your internet speed):
    ollama run qwythos-9b
  4. Fix Windows Blocking (CORS): If Luna says 'Connection Failed', Windows might be blocking it. Open Command Prompt as Administrator and run:
    setx OLLAMA_ORIGINS "*"
    Then restart your PC or completely close and reopen the Ollama app from the system tray.
  5. Configure Luna: Inside Luna, go to Settings (Gear Icon) -> Luna AI -> Enable "Use Local Model" and type qwythos-9b in the Model Name box.

LOCAL AI DIAGNOSTICS

Luna couldn't connect to your local Ollama server. Let's fix that!

1. Is Ollama Running?

Check your system tray (bottom right of Windows) for the Ollama alpaca icon. If it's not there, launch the Ollama app from your start menu.

2. Is the Model Pulled?

You must download the model before Luna can talk to it. Open your terminal and paste this command:

ollama pull qwythos-9b

3. Network & CORS

Sometimes your browser blocks the connection for security. To fix this on Windows, open Command Prompt as Administrator and run:

setx OLLAMA_ORIGINS "*"

Then completely close and restart Ollama from the system tray.
